channel.title: About on Nicholas Sizemore
channel.description: Recent content in About on Nicholas Sizemore

item.title: About
item.description: About Me This is a Hugo based resume template. You can find the full source code on GitHub. Research Interest Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Aliquam finibus ipsum ac erat aliquam dapibus. Vestibulum vehicula placerat ex, a consectetur odio pharetra quis1. Mauris id urna ante. Fusce pharetra diam ac nisi aliquet, velegestas ex iaculis. Pellentesque laoreet cursus tellus sed pellentesque. Praesent a rhoncus elit2. Nunc ipsum nisl, consequat sit amet pretium quis, gravida id ipsum.

item.title: Contact
item.description: Email: ncsizemore@uchicago.edu Mailing Address Knapp Center for Biomedical Discovery 900 East 57th St., KCBD #10128 Chicago, IL 60637

item.title: Projects
item.description: Current Projects Stochastic spread of HLB in Florida Analyzed and explicated the historical spread of citrus greening disease in Florida via stochastic dispersal mechanisms of varied temporal and spatial scales. Collaborated with domain experts at USDA and FDACS to develop a comprehensive spatially explicit, statewide-scale model. Implemented a robust computationally-efficient geo-spatial simulation platform using a combination of TensorFlow and R. Spectral conditions in nonconvex problems Motivated by problems in cancer diagnosis (and more general problems in data science/machine learning), we observe strong computational/empirical evidence for the existence of regimes in which there are provable, exact guarantees for the efficacy of natural spectral estimators as solutions to a class of discrete (nonconvex) optimization problems.

item.title: Research
item.description: Research Interests I have a fairly wide variety of mathematical interests. My research is typically centered around computational problems, and includes work on interdisciplinary applications and theoretical problems explored from a computational perspective. This often includes things like: Stochastic processes and applications Mathematical data analysis and machine learning A heavy dose of programming in various languages Current/ongoing projects are listed below; see the Projects page for more info.

item.title: Teaching
item.description: I am not currently teaching any courses. See below for past courses. University of Florida As a graduate student at UF, I taught classes as an instructor (responsible for all course content), and as a teaching assistant. Specific courses include: MAC 1140: Precalculus Algebra (instructor for both traditional and online) MAC 1147: Precalculus Algebra & Trigonometry (TA) MAC 2311: Calculus I (TA) MAC 2312: Calculus II (TA) MAC 2313: Calculus III (traditional TA; instructor online) Western Carolina University
